meaning: adj.冒烟的,烟状的,烟熏味的
examples: The ham had a smoky flavour.
His main problem was the extremely smoky atmosphere at work.
The fish had just the right amount of smoky flavour for my taste.
Camel milk, at least the one I try, is a little on the smoky side.
Folk events, such as grand, smoky festivals for the gods, have also regained momentum (or more accurately, funding).
